publication/title: Validation of an Artificial Intelligence Algorithm for Diagnostic Prediction of Coronary Disease: Comparison with a Traditional Statistical Model
publication/authors: Correia, L; Lopes, D; Porto, JV; Lacerda, YF; Correia, VC; Bagano, GO; Pontes, BS; Melo, MHV; Silva, TE; Meireles, AC
publication/tags:
* Coronary Disease Prediction
* Machine Learning vs Logistic Regression
* Chest Pain Diagnosis
* Fisher's Linear Discriminant Analysis
* Decision Tree
* ROC Curve Analysis
* Hosmer-Lemeshow Calibration
dataset/provenance:
* **Data Source Name**:
  - Data Source Name: Prospective chest pain registry.
    - The dataset was collected from patients admitted to the coronary unit of the hospital with chest pain and suspected coronary artery disease (CAD) from September 2011 to November 2017.

* **Total Data Points**:
  - Total Data Points: N=962.
    - The dataset was divided into a derivation sample (N=641) and a validation sample (N=321).

* **Previous Use**:
  - Previous Use: No.
    - Not enough information is available to determine if this dataset has been used in previous publications or benchmarks.

evaluation/method:
* Evaluation Strategy: Independent hold-out sets.
  The study used the first two-thirds of patients to build two probabilistic models of coronary disease: a machine learning algorithm and a traditional logistic model. The performance of these two predictive strategies was evaluated in the remaining third of patients.
evaluation/measure:
**Performance Metrics:**

* **Machine Learning Model:**
  * Area Under the Curve (AUC) - ROC: 0.81 (95% CI = 0.77 – 0.86)
  * Calibration: Hosmer-Lemeshow test (chi-square = 12.9, p = 0.11)
  * Linear Regression: Intercept = -0.119 (95% CI = -0.296 – 0.059), Slope = 1.228 (95% CI = 0.909 – 1.547), r = 0.953

* **Logistic Regression Model:**
  * Area Under the Curve (AUC) - ROC: 0.82 (95% CI = 0.78 – 0.87)
  * Calibration: Hosmer-Lemeshow test (chi-square = 6.2, p = 0.62)
  * Linear Regression: Intercept = 0.010 (95% CI = -0.083 – 0.103), Slope = 1.004 (95% CI = 0.840 – 1.168), r = 0.981
evaluation/comparison:
* **Comparison Performed**: Yes
  * Logistic Regression
    * The machine learning model was compared to a logistic regression model. The logistic regression model was built using the first two-thirds of patients and evaluated on the remaining third. The final logistic regression model included only significant variables at the 5% significance level.
  * Hosmer-Lemeshow Test
    * Both models were validated using the Hosmer-Lemeshow test for calibration. The logistic regression model showed a lower significance level for the difference between predicted and observed values (chi-square = 6.2; p = 0.62) compared to the machine learning model (chi-square = 12.9; p = 0.11), suggesting better calibration.
  * ROC Curve Analysis
    * The area under the ROC curve (AUC) was used to compare the discrimination performance of the two models. The AUC for the machine learning model was 0.81 (95% CI = 0.77 – 0.86), similar to the AUC for the logistic regression model, which was 0.82 (95% CI = 0.77 – 0.87), with a p-value of 0.68 indicating no significant difference.
